NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED GRANTING DEFENDANT FOLEY CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF (TRANSACTION ID # 62822900) FILED BY DEFENDANT FOLEY CONSTRUCTION

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT FOLEY CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF JOSE RAMIREZ IS GRANTED. GIVEN THE ALLEGED NATURE OF THE SERIOUS ONGOING PHYSICAL INJURIES RESULTING IN DISABILITY, AND THE PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DISCLOSURE OF MULTIPLE TREATING PAIN MANAGEMENT SPECIALISTS, THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE FOR A PHYSICAL EXAMINATION BY A PAIN MANAGEMENT PHYSICIAN. THE EXAM SHALL OCCUR ON JANUARY 7. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, OR ANOTHER OBSERVER SUCH AS A NURSE, AND/OR A COURT REPORTER MAY ATTEND AND RECORD THE EXAM VIA AUDIO OR STENOGRAPHIC MEANS. THE EXAMINING PHYSICIAN, DR. JAYSON HYMES, SHALL NOT REPEAT ANY QUESTIONING OF THE PLAINTIFF CONCERNING HOW THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT HAPPENED IF THIS HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN COVERED IN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES GEERHART; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Jose Ramirez
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Advanced From Jan-18-19 To Jan-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Jose Ramirez
Off Calendar Jan-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alex Halkias, Esq., L:aw Offices of Noel Hibbard, Telephone 408-294-9100, appearing for Plaintiff
David L. Crowe, Esq., Michael & Fackler, Telephone 925-933-7309, appearing for Defendant Foley Construction;

ARGUED: The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FOLEY CONSTRUCTION'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Jose Ramirez.

Granted. Given the alleged nature of the serious ongoing physical injuries resulting in disability, and the plaintiff's expert disclosure of multiple treating pain management specialists, the Court finds good cause for a physical examination by a pain management physician. The exam shall occur on January 7. Plaintiff's counsel, or another observer such as a nurse, and/or a court reporter may attend and record the exam via audio or stenographic means. The examining physician, Dr. Jayson Hymes, shall not repeat any questioning of the plaintiff concerning how the subject accident happened if this has previously been covered in plaintiff's deposition. Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Rafael Crespo Jr., Tel. No. 1-415-285-2063 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Lisa K. Thompson, Tel. No. 1-925-933-7309 appearing for the defendant
Foley Construction.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Defendant Foley Construction's motion for summary judgment is denied. Foley fails to satisfy its initial summary judgment burden to show that its exercise of retained control of the premises did not affirmatively contribute to Mr. Rodriquez's claimed injuries and, even if it did, there is a triable dispute whether its exercise of retained control affirmatively contributed to Mr. Rodriquez's claimed injuries. (Hooker v. Department of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198, 212, fn.3, and 214.) Fairly read, the complaint seeks to impose liability based on the exception to the Privette doctrine articulated in Hooker. The evidence on this motion also demonstrates that Foley constructed the elevator shaft and failed to ensure that safety measures were followed to prevent persons from falling down the shaft. (Foley Depo. 43:13-14; 58:21-59:25; 61:9-62:5; 84:10-13; 86:19-24; Rodriguez Dec. par. 5.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Foley Construction To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Foleys Form Interrogatories Set One Special Interrogatories Set One And To Produce Documents Or Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Set One And To Provide Verifications
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Rafael Crespo of the law offices of Rafael Crespo Jr. (415-285-2063) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Lisa K. Thompson of the law offices of Michel & Fackler (925-933-7309) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Stipulation to judge pro tem is signed and filed. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT FOLEY CONSTRUCTION'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Foleys Form Interrogatories Set One, Special Interrogatories Set One, And To Produce Documents Or Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Set One, And To Provide Verifications is GRANTED in part; DENIED in part. Plaintiff shall provide verifications to all responses served even if amended responses were subsequently served. Plaintiff shall provide a further verified amended response to Form Interrogatory No. 14.1 which states which specific safety codes and Cal-Osha laws Foley allegedly violated and deleting the statement "though it is obvious said laws have been violated." Motion denied as to Special Interrogatory No. 16 as Plaintiff has stated he doesn't recall any other medical providers. Defendants may conduct follow up discovery to test Plaintiff's assertion. Plaintiff to provide the supplemental response to No. 14.1 and all verifications by November 17, 2017. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: David R. McDonald; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
